description: You are a marine biologist at the Lizard Island Research Station on the Great Barrier Reef. To brief your dive team for the annual mass coral spawning, simulate sky conditions for a night dive on December 2, 2023, at 11:00 UTC. Set the simulation location to Lizard Island (14.668° S, 145.459° E, 0 m) with an 'Ocean' landscape. Ensure the Azimuthal grid and Atmosphere are enabled. Capture screenshots of the Moon and Crux (the Southern Cross) centered in the view. Save the current program configuration. Finally, create a dive plan document at /home/ga/Desktop/dive_plan.txt containing the location name 'Lizard Island' and references to the 'Moon' and 'Crux'.
